description: A VS Code extension with multiple color themes, animations, sounds, and icon themes inspired by Minecraft.

features.name: extension_scaffold
features.title: Extension Scaffold & Package Configuration
features.description: Set up the VS Code extension scaffold: package.json with activation events, commands, configuration properties, keybindings, and marketplace metadata. Convert from the Half-Life theme template.
acceptance: package.json has correct name, displayName, publisher, and description for Minecraft Theme
activationEvents includes onStartupFinished
contributes.themes array is empty (themes will be added in later features)
contributes.iconThemes array is empty (will be added later)
contributes.configuration has master sound toggle and per-event sound toggles
contributes.commands has enableAllSounds and disableAllSounds
contributes.keybindings remaps ctrl+j to a toggle-panel-with-sound command
contributes.markdown.previewStyles points to styles/minecraft-markdown.css
contributes.walkthroughs has a Minecraft-themed welcome walkthrough
vsce package produces a valid .vsix file

features.name: base_minecraft_theme
features.title: Base Minecraft Color Theme
features.description: Create the main 'Minecraft (General)' VS Code color theme. A dark theme inspired by the general Minecraft aesthetic: grass greens, stone grays, sky blues, wood browns. Define all editor colors using VS Code theme color references.
acceptance: Theme file exists at minecraft-theme/themes/Minecraft-color-theme.json
Defines editor.background, editor.foreground, sideBar.background, activityBar.background, titleBar.activeBackground, etc.
Uses Minecraft-inspired palette: grass greens (#4B7B3E, #5B8C4E), stone grays (#3C3C3C, #505050), sky blues (#4A9BE8), wood browns (#6B4226)
tokenColors define syntax highlighting for JS, TS, CSS, HTML, JSON, Markdown
Theme is registered in package.json contributes.themes
vsce package includes the theme file

features.name: biome_themes
features.title: Biome-Based Theme Variants
features.description: Create additional color themes inspired by Minecraft biomes: Plains, Forest, Desert, Nether, The End, and Ocean. Each has its own unique palette while maintaining readability.
acceptance: Theme files exist for at least 4 biomes: Plains, Desert, Nether, The End
Plains theme: warm greens, light blues, golden yellows
Desert theme: sandy browns, terracotta oranges, arid yellows
Nether theme: deep reds, oranges, netherrack browns, soulfire blues
End theme: dark purples, endstone off-whites, chorus plant pinks
Each theme defines the same set of required color keys as the base theme
Each theme has appropriate tokenColors for syntax highlighting
All themes are registered in package.json contributes.themes

features.name: audio_engine
features.title: Minecraft Sound Effects Engine
features.description: Implement the audio playback engine in extension.js. Replace Half-Life sounds with Minecraft sounds for events: startup (Cave Sound / disc_13), file save (item_pickup), file create (block_placed), file delete (block_break), terminal open (chest_open), terminal error (creeper_hiss), tab switch (click), file open (door_open), file close (door_close).
acceptance: extension.js has a sound map object with Minecraft sound files for each event
Linux uses mpg123, macOS uses afplay, Windows uses PowerShell audio
Dependency check runs on activation for the appropriate platform player
Each sound event can be individually toggled via VS Code configuration
Master enableSounds toggle controls all sounds
enableAllSounds and disableAllSounds commands work
togglePanelWithSound command wraps workbench.action.togglePanel with sound
No console.log in production code (use vscode.window for diagnostics)
Sound prioritization prevents overlapping critical sounds

features.name: minecraft_audio_assets
features.title: Minecraft Audio Asset Integration
features.description: Source and integrate Minecraft sound effect .mp3 files into minecraft-theme/audio/. Replace all Half-Life sounds with Minecraft equivalents.
acceptance: Audio directory contains Minecraft sounds: cave_sound.mp3, item_pickup.mp3, block_placed.mp3, block_break.mp3, chest_open.mp3, creeper_hiss.mp3, click.mp3, door_open.mp3, door_close.mp3
Each sound file is valid .mp3 format
Sounds are appropriately short (<5 seconds each for UI events)
File sizes are reasonable (<500KB each)
All sound references in extension.js match actual file names

features.name: faction_character_themes
features.title: Faction & Character Theme Variants
features.description: Create additional themes for Minecraft factions and characters: Villagers, Piglins, Endermen, Steve, and a Creeper theme.
acceptance: Villager theme: warm browns, emerald greens, parchment off-whites
Piglin theme: crimson reds, gold yellows, netherrack browns
Enderman theme: deep purples/black, ender pearl cyan highlights
Steve theme: classic blue jeans, cyan shirt, skin tones
Creeper theme: dark greens, black, creeper-face lime green accents
Each theme defines full editor color keys and tokenColors
All themes registered in package.json contributes.themes

features.name: minecraft_markdown_styles
features.title: Minecraft Markdown Preview Styles
features.description: Create custom CSS for VS Code's markdown preview that styles it like Minecraft's enchanting language or a written book.
acceptance: minecraft-markdown.css exists in minecraft-theme/styles/
CSS styles blockquotes like Minecraft achievement popups
CSS styles code blocks like Minecraft command blocks
CSS styles headings with Minecraft-inspired fonts and colors
CSS is registered in package.json contributes.markdown.previewStyles
Styles don't break existing markdown rendering

features.name: welcome_walkthrough
features.title: Minecraft Welcome Walkthrough
features.description: Create an interactive VS Code walkthrough that welcomes users to the Minecraft Theme with a tutorial on selecting themes, enabling sounds, and applying icon themes.
acceptance: Walkthrough registered in package.json contributes.walkthroughs
Walkthrough ID is minecraft-walkthrough
At least 3 steps: theme selection, icon theme, and sound enable
Each step has a title, description, media (image), and command link
Walkthrough uses Minecraft-themed language ('Equip Your Tools', 'Craft Your Workspace')
Images exist in minecraft-theme/images/ for walkthrough media

features.name: extension_optimization
features.title: Extension Packaging & Optimization
features.description: Final optimization pass: verify .vscodeignore is correct, prune unused assets, verify vsce packaging works, add LICENSE, and finalize CHANGELOG.
acceptance: .vscodeignore excludes source maps, node_modules (except runtime deps), and dev files
vsce package produces a .vsix under 5MB
LICENSE file is MIT or appropriate
CHANGELOG.md documents all features
Extension activates without errors on clean install
No Half-Life references remain in code or comments
